calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #5, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-26-2020 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with Barbara Simon.

The Court stated that it is good to have a little more detail on the bill pay entries, that the extended bill pay entries will require more detail going forward, that it is not inclined to retroactively authorize Ms. Simon to do the taxes, and that if the trust is taken out of Court supervision, it would not give any prospective authority. The Court also questions whether it is appropriate to have the same person (Conservator/Trustee) do the taxes. Ms. Robello stated that the previous order gave authority for preparation of the taxes and for payment of up to $500.00 per tax return per year, and that if the Trust is removed from Court supervision, they can remove that request. Ms. Simon stated that when someone dies it makes sense to have the same person do the taxes because they have and know all of the information, and that if the trust is removed from Court supervision, she will get an agreement from all of the Beneficiaries. The Court explained to Mr. Randolph what it means to have the trust removed from Court supervision. Mr. Randolph stated that as long as he has the option to come back to Court, he is okay with the trust being removed from Court supervision. Ms. Robello stated that the fee issues are addressed in the supplement, that they are only holding some personal property, that the vast majority of cash in the estate is being distributed and that they are withdrawing the request for fees for the administrative task entry. The Fifth Account and Report of Successor Trustee, etc. is under submission.

Judge: Joseph M. Quinn. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: J. Singh. Not reported.

MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Sheila Robello for and with Barbara Simon (Trustee).

The sale was confirmed at $1,575,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed December 7, 2018. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). An additional bond in the amount of $1,152,000.00 is required.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Kabasares. Not reported.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #3, REPORT, FEES
Off Calendar Feb-22-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: The Following matters were heard together: PTR-10-293282 The Barbara J. Randolph Trust & PCN-10-293283 the conservatorship of Barbara Randolph.
Appearances: Kevin Lachona for Barbara Simon.

Mr. Lachona addressed the examiners notes stating that the issue regarding certain time entries of the estate billed in the conservatorship and entries regarding the allowance delivery explaining that there was no specific amount of time regarding the drop off of allowance, that it coincided with other transactions/visits, that it was not just time spent visiting the conservatee but combined with meetings or talking to the caregivers, that there are over 10 entries where this situation applies, that the fees should not be reduced for this, that some entries were estate issues only, pointed out 10/19/14, 2/10/15 & 10/9/15 and that Ms. Simon tries to arrange visits to take care of multiple tasks. Mr. Lachona addressed the issue of payment to Jane Bauer explaining that it is not duplicative time, that the caregivers are not allowed to spend more than 10% of their time on cleaning & food preparation, that Jane was hired to help with this, that her time is less expensive than Ms. Simon, that it is necessary for the benefit of the conservatee and things that the caregiver cannot do. Mr. Lachona further explained that there was an issue with the previous order in that the endorsed filed copies that he received showed that the accountings had been waived when the original order states that accountings are not waived and he supplied the court with a corrected figure of a misstated fee. The accounting petitions in both cases were taken under submission.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not Reported)

Petition For Court Supervision Of Conservatee'S Trust, To Confirm Vacancy In The Office Ot Trustee, And For The Appointment Of Successor Trustee.
Off Calendar Jul-08-2010 Text Ruling

Petition For Order Appointing Barbara R Simon As Successor Trustee, Bringing Trust Under Continuing Court Supervision; And Setting Trustees Bond
Off Calendar Jul-13-2010 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: This matter was called with case number PTR-10-293283 (Conservatorship of Barbara Randolph).

Appearances: Court-Appointed Counsel Lawrence Siracusa for Barbara Randolph (Conservatee). Sheila Robello for/with Barbara Simon (Proposed Conservator). Lillian Ng for/with Michael Randolph (Son/Proposed Conservator) Nelson Wild for/with Maureen Randolph (Objector/Daughter)

Ms. Robello presented argument in support of her client. Mr. Siracusa stated that his client did not wish to appear. The Court waived the Conservatee's appearance. Ms. Ng stated that her client is opposed to Ms. Simon and argued in support of her client or another professional fiduciary. Mr. Siracusa stated that his client does not wish to choose between her children, that she wants Ms. Simon appointed, that the doctor's recommendations support the exclusive medical authority, that if Ms. Simon is appointed then she should go back to 2004 to check out any transfers to either child by the Conservatee, that his client should stay at home, and that the children's conflicts should stay out of the home. The Court granted the petition appointing Barbara Simon as the Conservator of the Person and Estate of Barbara Joan Randolph with exclusive medical authority and appointing her as Successor Trustee of the Barbara Joan Randolph 1989 Trust (subject to review of the orders).

Further orders (per written order):
1. Compliance Status Hearing - October 20, 2010, 9:00 a.m., Dept. 204:
2. Assessment Fee - $725 is charged.
3. First Account & Status Report - filed one week before September 14, 2011 (also for the Trust).
4. Ms. Simon is look into any transfers of $150.00 or more to either child starting in 2004.
5. No revisions to the Trust. Bond set at $90,000.00 in case #293282 and $220,000.00 in case #293282. Mr. Siracusa to remain on for 4 months.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Court Investigator: Molly Glocker. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy V. Jung.
